originalTitle: Nigeria warns its citizens in South Africa to be cautious after march turns violent
title: Being Kind and Careful in South Africa!
content:
Hello from your Africa News Friend!

Hi everyone! It's me, your friendly news reporter for Africa! I have a special story for you today. It's about being kind, being careful, and living together. We live on a big continent called Africa. Many different people live here. We all share this amazing home. Sometimes, people have different ideas. It is good to talk about our ideas.

A Special New Leader

In a place called East London, in South Africa, something happened. A very special man named Mr. Solomon Ogbonna Eziko had a big day. He was chosen to be a leader. He became an "Igbo leader." This means he would help and guide many people. It was a day for celebration for many. People like leaders because they help their communities. Leaders help make sure everyone is okay. They try to solve problems.

Who are the Igbo People?

You might be wondering, "Who are the Igbo people?" Well, the Igbo people are a special group. They are from a country called Nigeria. Nigeria is another country in Africa. Just like you might have a big family, the Igbo people are like a very big family. They share stories and traditions. They often live in many different places. Some Igbo people live in South Africa, too. It's nice to have leaders who understand your ways.

Why Some People Were Upset

Even though it was a happy day for Mr. Eziko and the Igbo people, some other people were not so happy. They had different ideas. They thought maybe it wasn't a good idea for him to be a leader there. Sometimes, people disagree. It's normal to have different opinions. But it's important to talk about them nicely. We should always try to understand each other.

What is a 'March'?

Because some people were upset, they decided to have a "march." A march is when many people walk together. They walk to show what they believe in. They want to tell everyone their ideas. Usually, a march is very peaceful. People walk and hold signs. They might sing or chant. They hope that leaders will listen to their message. **Did you know?** Marches have happened all over Africa for many important reasons! People marched to ask for freedom and fairness.

When Things Got a Bit Rough

Sadly, during this march, things got a bit rough. What started as a walk to share ideas became a bit scary. There was some pushing and shouting. This is what we call "violence." Violence means people are not being kind or gentle. They are hurting each other or breaking things. This is never a good thing. We should always try to be peaceful. We should use our words, not our hands, to solve problems.

Nigeria Says: Be Careful!

Because things got rough, the country of Nigeria sent an important message. They said, "To all our people from Nigeria who are living in South Africa, please be careful!" They told them to be cautious. Being cautious means you need to be extra careful. You need to look out for yourself and your friends. Nigeria wants its people to be safe. It's like your parents telling you to be careful when you cross the road. They want you to be okay. **Did you know?** Nigeria is the country with the most people in all of Africa! It's a very big country.

Being Safe and Smart

So, what does it mean to be careful? It means to listen to the news. It means to stay away from places where many people are shouting. If you see something that looks scary, tell a grown-up right away. Your parents, teachers, or other adults are there to help you. Always listen to their advice. They want to keep you safe and sound. It's good to be aware of what is happening around you. We all want to live in peace.

Being Good Neighbours

Africa is home to so many different people. We have different languages, different cultures, and different ideas. This is what makes Africa so special and colourful! But it's super important for everyone to be good neighbours. Being a good neighbour means being kind. It means respecting everyone. It means listening to each other, even if you don't agree. We should always try to find ways to live together happily. We can share our traditions. We can learn from each other. **Did you know?** There are over 2,000 different languages spoken across Africa! Imagine all the ways people can say "hello"!

Let's All Be Friends!

This story reminds us of something very important. We need to treat everyone with kindness. We need to be careful when things get noisy or rough. And we need to always try to understand each other. No matter where we come from, we are all people. We all deserve to feel safe and happy. Let's promise to be kind friends to everyone. We can make our towns and cities wonderful places for all.
summary: In South Africa, a new leader caused some people to get upset, and after a walk became rough, the country of Nigeria told its citizens there to be extra careful.
whyMatters: It's important because it reminds us to be kind to everyone, even if they are different, and to be careful when things get scary.

originalTitle: Tuanzebe sends DR Congo back to World Cup after 52 years
title: Tuanzebe sends DR Congo back to World Cup after 52 years
content: DR Congo qualify for their first World Cup since 1974 as Axel Tuanzebe's strike gives the Leopards a 1-0 win over Jamaica in their play-off.

originalTitle: Sexual violence part of 'everyday life' in parts of Sudan, charity says
title: Helping Our Friends in Sudan Stay Safe
content:
Hello from Your Kid Reporter in Africa!

Hi everyone! It's me, your kid news reporter, here to tell you about something important happening in our big, beautiful continent of Africa. Today, we're talking about a country called Sudan. It's a place with lots of wonderful people, just like you and me. We want everyone, everywhere, to feel safe and happy. That's a super important thing, isn't it?

Feeling Safe is Super Important!

Think about how you feel when you are playing in your garden or at school with your friends. You feel safe, right? You know that grown-ups are looking after you. Feeling safe means you can play, learn, and grow without being scared. It's a warm, good feeling that everyone in the world should have, no matter where they live. Everyone deserves to feel safe and loved.

What's Happening in Sudan?

Now, in some parts of Sudan, things are a little tricky right now. Some grown-ups and even children are not always feeling safe. It makes them feel sad and scared. People are sometimes getting hurt or treated badly when they are just trying to do their normal daily things. This is a big problem because everyone should feel safe to live their lives. Did you know? Sudan is a very, very big country! It's so big that it has lots of different places and people with different cultures. It's in the northeastern part of Africa, and it has a long, interesting history.

Everyday Things Can Be Hard

Imagine you want to go to the shop to buy some bread, or you need to walk to get water for your family. Or maybe you just want to go outside and play with your friends. These are normal, everyday things, right? But for some people in Sudan, doing these simple things has become hard. They worry that they might not be safe when they go outside. This makes daily life very difficult and scary for them. When people can't do everyday things because they are scared, it means they need help. It's like if you couldn't go to school because you felt unsafe – it would make you very sad and stop you from learning. We want our friends in Sudan to be able to do their everyday things without any fear. Did you know? In many parts of Africa, people, especially women and children, walk long distances every day to get water for their families. It's a very important job!

Who is Helping Our Friends?

Good news! There are kind people who want to help. A special group called a 'charity' is working very hard in Sudan. A charity is like a team of superheroes who work together to help people who are in trouble or need extra care. They don't wear capes, but they do amazing things to make the world a better place. This charity is listening to the people in Sudan and working to make them feel safe again. They hear stories from people who have been hurt or felt unsafe. Then, the charity tries to find ways to protect these people and make sure they get the help they need to feel better. They want to make sure that no one has to feel scared while doing simple things like getting water or going to the market. Did you know? There are thousands of charities all over the world! They help with all sorts of things, like giving food to people who are hungry, building schools, and helping animals. They all work to make the world a kinder place.

Why Do Charities Help?

Charities help because they believe that everyone deserves to be treated kindly and to live in safety. They know that when people are hurt or scared, it makes them feel very sad inside. The charity wants to bring back smiles and happiness to the faces of the people in Sudan. They want to make sure everyone knows they are cared for and that bad things should not happen to them. It's like when your friend is sad, and you try to cheer them up or help them. Charities do that on a bigger scale! They are there to show people that they are not alone and that help is on the way. They give support and comfort to those who have been through tough times. Did you know? Helping others makes you feel good too! When you do something kind for someone else, it makes your heart feel warm and happy inside.

What Can We Do?

Even though we might be far away, we can still do something! We can learn about what's happening and understand that some people need our thoughts and kindness. If you ever feel unsafe, or if you see someone else who is not feeling safe, the best thing to do is tell a trusted grown-up, like your parent, teacher, or another family member. They are there to help you! Knowing that people are working to help our friends in Sudan is also important. It shows that even when things are tough, there are always good people trying to make things better. We can hope and wish for peace and safety for everyone in Sudan. Every little bit of kindness and understanding helps! Did you know? Talking about your feelings, even when they are sad or scary, can help you feel better. A trusted adult can always help you think through things.

Working Together for a Brighter Day

So, remember, everyone, including our friends in Sudan, deserves to feel safe and happy every single day. The charity is working hard to make sure this happens. We can all learn from this and remember to be kind to everyone we meet. Let's send our happy thoughts and hopes for safety to all the children and grown-ups in Sudan, wishing them a brighter, safer day very soon! Thank you for listening, and stay tuned for more news from your kid reporter in Africa!
summary: A children's charity is working in Sudan to help people, especially kids, feel safe and happy again after facing difficult and scary situations while doing everyday activities.
whyMatters: It matters because everyone, everywhere, deserves to feel safe and happy, and right now, some people in Sudan are not feeling safe, so good people are helping them.

originalTitle: Bosnia fans celebrate as team qualifies for World Cup, knocking Italy out
title: Bosnia fans celebrate as team qualifies for World Cup, knocking Italy out
content: Supporters celebrated as Bosnia and Herzegovina beat Italy in a penalty shootout to secure a spot in the 2026 World Cup.

content: Defence Minister Israel Katz also says houses in Lebanese villages near the Israeli border will be demolished.

originalTitle: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad
title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad
content: The US state department says a suspect in her abduction has ties to an Iran-backed militia group, Kataib Hezbollah.

originalTitle: Failed experiment as England get grim glimpse of life without Kane
title: Failed experiment as England get grim glimpse of life without Kane
content: England's World Cup hopes rest on captain Harry Kane after a grim taste of life without him in the defeat against Japan at Wembley, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

originalTitle: 'Scotland fans can fret - but they need to keep perspective too'
title: 'Scotland fans can fret - but they need to keep perspective too'
content: There is a section of Scotland supporters who have taken to booing the head coach and the team. With a World Cup coming, they need to keep perspective, writes Tom English.

originalTitle: Angry Wales boss Bellamy shows his new old self
title: Angry Wales boss Bellamy shows his new old self
content: Craig Bellamy showed his fiery temper for the first time as Wales boss in their Northern Ireland draw - a rage that was evident during his playing days.
